layman_headline: Crypto market is scared and no coin is screaming 'buy me' right now.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in a 'Fear' state with Bitcoin dominance high at 56.5%, meaning money is flowing away from smaller coins and into Bitcoin. All of the swing trade candidates have significant problems: some are deeply oversold but still falling hard (BONK), some have already run up too much (DEXE with RSI 85, LDO up 23% this week), and others lack enough data to make a confident call. Ethereum has a low RSI near oversold territory and is the strongest large-cap alt, but its chart structure is still bearish with price below all major moving averages. The best move right now is patience.
layman_action: Wait for a clearer setup. Keep cash ready. If ETH drops to around $1,780 and shows a bounce with volume confirmation, that could be the entry. Don't chase anything in a fearful, Bitcoin-dominant market.
expert_reasoning: No candidate presents a high-conviction swing entry right now. ETH (RSI 35, reliable signal per our track record) is the closest to actionable, but the chart bias is neutral with price below all MAs in a downtrend structure. The risk/reward to nearest levels is only 0.36x which is poor for entry. BTC dominance at 56.5% is a headwind for all altcoins. The Fear & Greed index at 27 is improving from 25 but not yet at extreme fear (sub-20) levels that historically mark strong bounce zones. DEXE has a bullish chart but RSI at 85 is dangerously overbought — this is a sell signal, not a buy. LDO's 23.5% weekly surge makes it extended despite the low RSI. Best to wait for ETH to test $1,700-1,780 support with a reversal candle before entering.
risk_factors: Iran strikes escalation could trigger broader risk-off selling, dragging crypto further down
BTC dominance at 56.5% and rising means altcoin capital is being drained — all alt trades carry extra risk
Fear & Greed at 27 is 'Fear' but not 'Extreme Fear' — we could see another leg down before a true bottom
watch_list: ETH price action around $1,700-1,780 support zone — a bounce with volume would trigger a BUY signal
Fear & Greed index — if it drops below 20 (Extreme Fear), that historically marks better entry points
BTC price holding $62,000 — a break below could accelerate altcoin selling
market_context: Crypto market is in Fear mode (27/100) with BTC dominance elevated at 56.5%, pressuring altcoins. Geopolitical tensions (Iran strikes) are adding to risk-off sentiment, and no clear reversal signals have materialized yet.
context_banner: Market fear is rising but hasn't reached extreme levels — patience is the highest-conviction trade right now.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 35 is approaching oversold but not yet at the 30 threshold that our track record shows as a reliable buy signal (80% win rate). Waiting for sub-30 or a confirmed bounce.
Fear & Greed at 27 improving from 25 suggests sentiment is bottoming but hasn't reached extreme fear levels that produce the best contrarian entries.
Bitcoin price sagging under $62.5K due to Iran strikes adds geopolitical overhang — a reliable news source (67% win rate) flagging bearish pressure.
ETH derivatives reset article suggests leveraged positions are being cleared, which could create a cleaner setup for entry once selling exhausts.
No candidate has both a bullish chart bias AND favorable RSI — DEXE is bullish but RSI 85 is dangerous, ETH has good RSI but bearish structure. Conflicting signals warrant waiting.
